source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Hint: Please answer the question requiring a floating-point number with one decimal place and provide the final value, e.g., 1.2, 1.3, 1.4, at the end.
Question: The Fourier transform of a signal x_a(t) is given in <image> (a). This signal passes through the system in <image> (b). where $x_c[n]=x_b(n T)$ and
$$
x_e(t)=\sum_{n=-\infty}^{\infty} x_d[n] \delta(t-n T)
$$
and
$$
H(j \omega)= \begin{cases}T & \text { if }|\omega|<\frac{\pi}{T} \\ 0 & \text { otherwise }\end{cases}
$$
a. Tetermine an expression for $X_f(j \omega)$ for when
0<\omega<\pi / 2 \text { : }
$$
extraction_notes: image field is a PIL.Image object representation, not a path/URL, so image_paths is empty

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says the Fourier transform is given in figure (a) and the system is in figure (b)
the asked quantity depends on spectral content and system behavior that are only partially specified in text and likely completed by the figure
multi_step: the pipeline references multiple signals and stages: x_a(t) -> system -> x_b(t) -> sampling x_c[n]=x_b(nT) -> discrete-time stage x_d[n] -> impulse train x_e(t) -> filter H(jω) -> x_f(t)
the task asks for X_f(jω) over a frequency interval, suggesting spectral transformation across several operations rather than direct lookup
solving likely requires combining continuous-time Fourier analysis, sampling/aliasing reasoning, and ideal low-pass reconstruction
verifiability: answer_present=true and answer_type=number, so there is a concrete target output recorded
registry_passed=true and no asset issues are listed
however, the question wording asks for an expression for X_f(jω), while the stored answer is a single numeric value '0', which may make strict verification less straightforward
full verification appears to require the figure content because the defining spectrum is said to be in figure (a)

decision_reasons: question, primary image, and answer are all present, so the sample has the core semantic assets needed for recovery and evaluation
the problem appears strongly image-dependent and the referenced figure asset exists, which supports preserving it in the candidate pool
initial scoring suggests substantial reasoning complexity, making it a potentially valuable candidate
there is some verification risk because the question appears to ask for an expression while the stored answer is a single number, so priority is moderated rather than maximal
